table.paper_name: CRM: Single Image to 3D Textured Mesh with Convolutional Reconstruction Model

Recognizing the limitations posed by sparse 3D data, we highlight the necessity of integrating geometric priors into network design. we present the Convolutional Reconstruction Model (CRM), a high-fidelity feed-forward single image-to-3D generative model. table.paper_name: RAG-Gym: Systematic Optimization of Language Agents for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
table.authors_name: Guangzhi Xiong, Qiao Jin, Xiao Wang, Yin Fang, Haolin Liu, Yifan Yang
table.summary_full_paper: often depend on ad-hoc prompt engineering and lack a unified optimization framework. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has shown great promise for knowledge-intensive tasks and recently advanced with agentic RAG, where language agents engage in multi-round interactions with external knowledge sources. we propose Re$^2$Search, a novel agent incorporating reasoning reflection that significantly outperforms standard prompts. In actor tuning, we evaluate three popular post-training algorithms with fine-grained process sup. For prompt engineering, we propose Re$^2$Search, a novel agent incorporating reasoning reflection that significantly outperforms standard prompts.
table.problem_solved: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has shown great promise for knowledge-intensive tasks and recently advanced with agentic RAG, where language agents engage in multi-round interactions with external knowledge sources for adaptive information retrieval.
table.proposed_model_or_approach: The paper describes its empirical setting in the source text. The source metadata does not expose a distinct named model. It proposes or applies we propose Re$^2$Search, a novel agent incorporating reasoning reflection that significantly outperforms standard prompts. In actor tuning, we evaluate three popular post-training algorithms with fine-grained process.

table.paper_name: MultiHop-RAG: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Multi-Hop Queries
table.summary_full_paper: perform unsatisfactorily in retrieving and answering multi-hop queries. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) augments large language models (LLM) by retrieving relevant knowledge, showing promising potential in mitigating LLM hallucinations and enhancing response quality, thereby facilitating the great adoption. We hope MultiHop-RAG will be a valuable resource for the community in developing effective RAG systems, thereby facilitating greater adoption of LLMs in practice.
table.problem_solved: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) augments large language models (LLM) by retrieving relevant knowledge, showing promising potential in mitigating LLM hallucinations and enhancing response quality, thereby facilitating the great adoption of LLMs in practice.
table.proposed_model_or_approach: The paper studies the dataset or experimental setting described as Furthermore, to our knowledge, no existing RAG benchmarking dataset focuses on multi-hop queries.. It uses GPT as a central model component. It proposes or applies However, we find that existing RAG systems are inadequate in answering multi-hop queries, which require retrieving and reasoning over multiple pieces of supporting evidence..
